source_title: DOW-UAP-D57, Range Fouler Reporting Form, Gulf of Aden, September 2020
description_blurb:
This document is a Range Fouler Reporting Form, a standardized reporting form the U.S. Navy uses to record the circumstances surrounding an unauthorized intrusion into controlled airspace during active military operations or training. These reports contain a narrative description of the observer’s experiences. 
A U.S. military operator reported tracking a “round, cold object” over the Gulf of Aden for eight minutes via “black hot” IR sensor, making the UAP appear “bright white.” The report states that the UAP was “traveling 168 degrees at 277 mph” and “made a few abrupt directional changes” during the encounter.
All descriptive and estimative language contained in this report reflects the reporter’s subjective interpretation at the time of the event. Such characterizations should not be interpreted as a conclusive indication of the presence or absence of any intrinsic object features or performance characteristics.
title: DOW-UAP-D57, Range Fouler Reporting Form, Gulf of Aden, September 2020
summary: A U.S. military operator reported tracking a "round, cold object" over the Gulf of Aden for eight minutes (21:09z to 21:17z on 2020-09-04) at an altitude of 23,819 HAT. The object was tracked via an IR sensor set to "black hot," appearing "bright white." It was traveling 168 degrees at 277 mph and made "a few abrupt directional changes" during the encounter. This report is a standardized Range Fouler Reporting Form used by the U.S. Navy.
reported_object_description: A round, cold object that appeared bright white on a black hot IR sensor.
reported_behavior: Traveling 168 degrees at 277 mph and making a few abrupt directional changes.
bust_reasoning: The bust score of 45 reflects genuine uncertainty. The object was tracked by a U.S. military operator via an IR sensor for 8 minutes, exhibiting a speed of 277 mph and making 'abrupt directional changes.' The description 'round, cold object' (bright white on black hot IR) combined with these kinematics resists an easy mundane explanation. However, with only a single sensor modality (IR) and no stable trackfile or visual confirmation, a mundane explanation such as a misidentified drone or aircraft with unusual maneuvers and IR signature remains plausible without being definitively ruled out by the document.
full_text:
# DOW-UAP-D57, Range Fouler Reporting Form, Gulf of Aden, September 2020 - Page 1

Declassified by MG Richard A. Harrison
USCENTCOM Chief of Staff
Declassified on 16 March 2026
FOUO/Privacy Act applies
(see below).

# Range Fouler Reporting Form

Please complete this form to the best of your ability. If you do not have the requested information, please leave the field blank. If there was more than one "group," please report each on a separate form for data collection purposes. You should receive a response within 5 business days.

| Last Name, First Name | Rank | Squadron | SIPR Email Address | Crew Position |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| [redacted] | O-3 | 172 ATKS | [redacted] | Other |

This information is for contact only. SPEAR sanitizes all reports of identifying information. Absolutely no identifying information for aircrew or squadron will be recorded for analysis.

| Date (mm/dd/yy) | Time of detection (hh:mm:ss Z) | Day / Night | Side No. | Buno | ISR | Mission Description (CAS, BFM, etc) | LFE? |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 09/04/20 | 21:09:00 | Night | | | | | |

| Contact Working Area (Ex: W-72 1A) | Contact Latitude (DD:MM:SS) | N/S | Contact Longitude (DD:MM:SS) | E/W | Contact Altitude (Ex: 22000) | Altitude Constant? | Wind Dir at Contact Alt (From) | Wind Speed |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| | | | | | 23819 | | | |

Please attempt to determine a Lat / Long for the contact at initial detection. We recommend using JMPS to reference the sensor-derived bearing and range relative to the bullseye you had in use. If that method is not available, any reasonable derivation of location will work, but please be as specific as possible and make a note in the comments section. These locations may be used to cue other means of tracking.

**Was the contact moving?** Yes
**Direction/Speed (Ex: 090/15)**: 168/277

---

**Radar Equipped:** [ ]
**Stable Trackfile?:** [ ]
**# of Contacts in "Group":** 1

**AIM-9x Self-Track:** [ ]
**ATFLIR Autotrack:** [ ]
**Tally Achieved:** [ ]

**EA Indications (Check all that apply):**
[ ] ECM Arc
[ ] Letter Identifier
[ ] False Trackfiles
[ ] Other/Ambiguous

**Please check all that apply:**
Round: [x]
Square: [ ]
Balloon-shaped: [ ]
Wings/Airframe: [ ]
Other Shape: [ ]
Apparent Propulsion: [ ]
Moving Parts: [ ]
Metallic: [ ]
Markings: [ ]
Translucent: [ ]
Opaque: [ ]
Reflective: [ ]

---

Please use the field below to describe the contact and any interaction in your own words with as much detail as possible. Please be sure to include any detail not included in questions above.

Contact at 21:09z to 21:17z 04SEP2020. MGRS location 38P LT 7[redacted] [redacted]. While at 23,819 HAT over the Gulf of Aden we tracked a round, cold object in IR traveling 168 degrees at 277 mph. It made a few abrupt directional changes during the 8 minute contact. Our sensor was aimed -39 degrees below our altitude with a slant range of 6.17NM and ground range of 8.81KM. The IR sensor was set to black hot and the object in question was a bright white.

---

Don't use the purple "submit" button! Save this form with filename "Date_Squadron_RF.pdf" and email it to [redacted] (Also in the global). For troubleshooting, call [redacted].

Thank you for your time. Please ensure all display tapes are ripped for the entire time of interaction and saved as a .wmv (Example: 4 May VFA-106 HUD.wmv). Squadron intel personnel shall upload those files to the repository located at this link.
[redacted]

USCENTCOM 26-0028
Approved for Release to AARO - FOUO / PA applies
03/16/26 000001
cover_up_reasoning: The document shows routine redaction of personal identifying information (names, email addresses) and partial operational details (MGRS location, submission contacts). It is marked 'FOUO/Privacy Act applies' and explicitly states that identifying information is sanitized for analysis, indicating a policy of privacy rather than concealment of the incident itself. There is no evidence of classified annexes, substantial black-bar redactions across multiple pages, or contradictions with public statements.
title_zh: DOW-UAP-D57，靶场干扰物报告表，亚丁湾，2020年9月
summary_zh: 一名美国军事操作员报告称，于2020年9月4日世界时21时09分至21时17分，在亚丁湾上空追踪到一个“圆形、低温物体”，高度为23,819 HAT，持续八分钟。该物体通过设置为“黑热”模式的红外传感器进行追踪，显示为“亮白色”。其以277英里/小时的速度沿168度方向飞行，并在遭遇过程中进行了“几次突然的方向改变”。本报告是美国海军使用的标准化靶场干扰物报告表。
reported_object_description_zh: 一个圆形、低温物体，在黑热红外传感器上显示为亮白色。
reported_behavior_zh: 以277英里/小时的速度沿168度方向飞行，并进行了几次突然的方向改变。
bust_reasoning_zh: 驳斥评分为45，反映出真实的不确定性。该物体由一名美国军事操作员通过红外传感器追踪了8分钟，表现出277英里/小时的速度并进行了“突然的方向改变”。“圆形、低温物体”（在黑热红外上显示为亮白色）的描述与这些运动学特征相结合，难以用简单的世俗解释。然而，仅凭单一传感器模式（红外）且没有稳定的追踪文件或视觉确认，诸如被误识别的无人机或具有异常机动和红外特征的飞机等世俗解释仍然合理，且未被文件明确排除。
cover_up_reasoning_zh: 该文件显示了对个人身份信息（姓名、电子邮件地址）的例行性编辑以及部分操作细节（MGRS位置、提交联系方式）。文件标有“FOUO/隐私法适用”字样，并明确指出身份信息已为分析目的进行清理，表明这是一项隐私政策，而非对事件本身的隐瞒。没有证据表明存在机密附件、跨多页的大面积黑条编辑，或与公开声明相矛盾之处。
claims: Object appeared "bright white" on "black hot" IR sensor
claims_zh: 物体在“黑热”红外传感器上显示为“亮白色”
物体以277英里/小时的速度飞行
物体进行了“突然的方向改变”
bust_explanations: A military drone or aircraft operating in the area. The 'round' shape could be a misidentification due to distance, angle, or sensor resolution. The 'cold' signature (bright white on black hot IR) is plausible for an object at high altitude or with specific thermal properties. The speed (277 mph) and 'abrupt directional changes' are within the capabilities of some known aircraft or drones, though 'abrupt' suggests unusual maneuvers.

An advanced surveillance balloon or similar atmospheric platform. While 277 mph and 'abrupt directional changes' are highly unusual for typical balloons, the 'round, cold' description fits. However, the reported kinematics make this a less likely explanation than a drone/aircraft.

A misidentified meteorological phenomenon or atmospheric effect. While the sustained tracking for 8 minutes with reported speed and directional changes makes this less likely, complex atmospheric conditions can sometimes create unusual sensor returns that are difficult to interpret.
bust_explanations_zh: 该区域内运行的军用无人机或飞机。“圆形”形状可能是由于距离、角度或传感器分辨率导致的误识别。“低温”特征（在黑热红外上显示为亮白色）对于高空物体或具有特定热特性的物体来说是合理的。速度（277英里/小时）和“突然的方向改变”在某些已知飞机或无人机的能力范围内，尽管“突然”暗示了异常机动。
先进的侦察气球或类似大气平台。虽然277英里/小时的速度和“突然的方向改变”对于典型气球来说极不寻常，但“圆形、低温”的描述是吻合的。然而，报告的运动学特征使得这比无人机/飞机更不可能成为解释。
被误识别的气象现象或大气效应。虽然持续追踪8分钟并报告了速度和方向改变使得这种可能性降低，但复杂的大气条件有时会产生难以解释的异常传感器回波。
cover_up_indicators: Redaction of personal identifying information (name, rank, email)
Redaction of operational contact information (email addresses for submission/troubleshooting)
cover_up_indicators_zh: 个人身份信息（姓名、军衔、电子邮件）的编辑
操作联系信息（提交/故障排除的电子邮件地址）的编辑
